content.full_text:
June 1, 1992

Blanca Breeze, Esq.
Staff Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814-0807

RE: Confirmation of June 1, 1992 Telephone Advice --
Regulation 18901 --
Inter-governmental mailings exemption

Dear Ms. Breeze:

Thank you for speaking with me on the telephone this afternoon. This letter requests confirmation of telephone advice that our office may mail copies of Mr. Sherman's May 29, 1992 San Francisco Chronicle article entitled "California Loses Millions in Court's Sales Tax Ruling", together with a letter urging enactment of the federal legislation described in the article, to California legislators, constitutional officers, U.S. Senators, and Members of Congress, and that such mailings to present officeholders would not be subject to the 200-per-month limit of Regulation 18901.

The basis for this advice is Regulation 18901, subdivision (b)(3), which exempts from the limit: "Any item sent in the normal course of business from one governmental entity or officer to another governmental entity or officer."

Thank you very much for your prompt attention to our request.

Sincerely,

Steven M. Kamp
